summary: Cross-chain mapping for the Multichain bridge benchmark. Ethereum was the largest source-chain custody holder (anyCallV6 router + older Multichain Router). Funds were drained to attacker addresses on Fantom (largest destination ~$122M), Avalanche, and Polygon. The mapping treats Fantom as the primary destination_chain; secondary AVAX/Polygon legs are listed under `secondary_chains` in metadata.json.

contracts.note: Fantom-side router (same address bytecode as Ethereum side, deployed at the same address on Fantom). Received the largest single drain destination — ~$122M of the total $126M loss landed in Fantom attacker accounts.

entity_map.role: Threshold Signature Scheme / Multi-Party Computation ceremony among federated nodes. Produced ONE aggregate ECDSA signature per cross-chain action. Modelled on-chain by a single signer (N=1, K=1) per the MPC simulation gap (see metadata.mpc_simulation).
anyCallV6 router on Ethereum — verified the post-MPC aggregate signature and dispatched unlock to the correct destination chain id.
Older Multichain Router on Ethereum, still active at the time of the incident — held additional locked balances drained alongside anyCallV6.
Fantom-side router — primary destination of the drain; held wrapped representations (multiUSDC, multiETH, etc.) of the source-chain custody.
On-chain witness for the post-MPC aggregate signature. EOA-shaped key per the simulation gap; the corresponding TSS administration is the off-chain artifact that broke.
Attacker EOA cluster on Fantom (and AVAX/Polygon) that received the 6 large drain transactions. Attribution officially unconfirmed.

asset_map.notes: Largest single asset drained — ~$53M. Destination token is multiUSDC on Fantom.
Second-largest asset — ~$21M. Destination token is multiETH on Fantom.
~$8M drained. Destination is wrapped BTC representation on Fantom.

fantom.note: Fantom-side counterpart received the wrapped representations as the primary destination of the drain.

message_map.message_id_field: digest = keccak256(abi.encode(target, value, data, nonce, address(this)))
message_map.proof_field: bytes[] sigs (exactly 1 ECDSA signature — the post-MPC aggregate)

invariants_for_fuzzer.description: Every Unlocked event must correspond to a prior `execute` call carrying exactly 1 valid signature from the configured post-MPC aggregate signer.

Only the address in the constructor's signer list (size 1) may contribute the aggregate signature. (On-chain enforced; off-chain assumption 'MPC administration is decentralised among >=2 principals' is what the incident broke.)
totalLocked[token] >= cumulative released[token]; bucket cannot release more than it has custodied for any single token, even across 6 batched receipts to multiple destination chains.
Each (target, value, data, nonce, bridge_address) tuple may unlock at most once — replay protection on the digest cache.

(Architectural / off-chain) The MPC ceremony's administration must require participation from >= 2 independent principals. Multichain's de-facto 1-of-1 administration is the structural precondition that the 2023-07-06 incident exploited; this invariant is *not* enforceable on-chain in any TSS protocol.
